spec_match.rationale: Page is explicitly framed around the chromestatus 'foundation for part-like pseudos' motivation, exercises ::first-line, ::selection, :placeholder-shown chains and lists the downstream features (::details-content, customizable <select>) the change unblocks.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised the three toggles, the shadow input, the live CSS readout, and the CSS.supports() probe panel. The probe now reports both supported chains and expected rejections.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index ('stack ::part() with ::first-line, ::selection, :placeholder-shown — chains rejected before Chrome 130') matches the page exactly.

edge_cases.rationale: The probe panel now contrasts supported ::part() chains with expected rejections for a combinator after ::part(), :has() after ::part(), and a second ::part() chain.

references.rationale: Links to both https://chromestatus.com/feature/5195333643272192 and the css-shadow-parts-1 spec.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, no static-card pattern, contrast ratios look fine (charcoal-on-paper, muted-on-stone are within AA).

openQuestions.title: Link to the live ::details-content and customizable <select> demos
openQuestions.detail: The page mentions these as the motivating downstream features but doesn't link out. If those features have their own concept pages on the site (Chrome 131 / 135), surface those links from the unblocks list so a visitor can navigate the dependency chain.

openQuestions.title: Wire a real <select> picker mock-up alongside the input
openQuestions.detail: The 'foundation' framing is strongest when the visitor can see the same selector syntax restyling a soon-to-ship picker. A small ::picker(select) mock-up (even unimplemented in current browsers but with a CSS.supports probe) would close the loop on the foundation argument.

summary: Re-reviewed in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp. The concept now shows seven live selector probes: four supported chains plus expected rejections for combinator, :has(), and chained ::part() selectors. DevTools verified all three toggles update the live CSS readout, the rejected rows stay rejected, the shadow input has id/name attributes, and console/network are clean. Two minor follow-ups remain for links to downstream feature demos and a picker mock-up.
